# Git Workflow & Convention

## Step 0: Check Project Git Config

Before any git operation, check `.context/INDEX.md` at the project root.
If it lists `.context/git-workflow.md`, read that file for:

```
main_branch:    main / master / trunk
strategy:       trunk-based / gitflow
ticket_prefix:  BNCP / JIRA / GH
tag_format:     v{semver}
```

If `.context/INDEX.md` or `.context/git-workflow.md` doesn't exist, ask:
- What is the main branch? (`main` / `master` / `trunk`)
- What branching strategy? (trunk-based / git flow)
- What is the ticket prefix? (e.g. `BNCP-`)

Use these values for all branch names, commit examples, and tag commands below.

---


## Table of Contents
1. Conventional Commits Format
2. Type Reference
3. Scope Guidelines

5. Git Flow Strategy

---

## 1. Conventional Commits Format

```
<type>(<scope>): <subject>

[optional body]

[optional footer]
```

**Rules:**
- Subject: imperative mood, lowercase, no period, max 72 chars
- Body: explain *what* and *why* — not *how*. Wrap at 72 chars
- Footer: breaking changes, issue references

```
feat(bot-builder): add voice script configuration panel

Allow users to configure TTS voice, speed, and language per bot.
Previously this required manual API calls.

Closes #234
```

---

## 2. Type Reference

| Type | When to Use | SemVer Bump |
|---|---|---|
| `feat` | New feature visible to users | Minor |
| `fix` | Bug fix | Patch |
| `perf` | Performance improvement, no behavior change | Patch |
| `refactor` | Code restructure, no behavior change | — |
| `style` | Formatting, whitespace, missing semicolons | — |
| `test` | Add or fix tests only | — |
| `docs` | Documentation only | — |
| `chore` | Build process, dependency updates, tooling | — |
| `ci` | CI/CD pipeline changes | — |
| `revert` | Revert a previous commit | — |

**Breaking change** — append `!` after type or add `BREAKING CHANGE:` in footer:
```
feat(api)!: remove deprecated v1 endpoints

BREAKING CHANGE: /api/v1/* endpoints removed. Migrate to /api/v2/*.
```

---

## 3. Scope Guidelines

Scope = the part of the codebase affected. Keep it consistent across the team.

```bash
# Features / modules (use your project's actual feature names)
feat(auth): ...
feat(dashboard): ...
feat(checkout): ...
feat(notifications): ...
feat(analytics): ...

# Layers
fix(api): ...
fix(ui): ...
fix(db): ...

# Infrastructure / tooling
chore(deps): ...
ci(deploy): ...
chore(docker): ...
```

**When to omit scope** — changes that truly span the whole codebase:
```
refactor: migrate all components to standalone
chore: upgrade Angular to v17
```

> **Project scopes:** Replace the examples above with your project's actual module names.
> Check `.context/git-workflow.md` for the agreed scope list. Add new scopes there when a new
> feature domain is introduced so the whole team uses the same vocabulary.

### Good vs Bad

```
✅ feat(auth): add OAuth2 social login support
✅ fix(dashboard): prevent crash when data is empty on first load
✅ refactor(user-service): replace BehaviorSubject with signal state
✅ chore(deps): upgrade @angular/core to 17.3.0
✅ ci(deploy): add staging environment step

❌ feat: add stuff
❌ fix: bug fix
❌ fixed the thing
❌ WIP
❌ misc changes
```

---

## 4. Branch Naming

```
<type>/<ticket-id>-<short-description>
```

| Type | When |
|---|---|
| `feat/` | New feature |
| `fix/` | Bug fix |
| `refactor/` | Refactor, no behavior change |
| `chore/` | Tooling, deps, config |
| `docs/` | Documentation |
| `hotfix/` | Production emergency |

```bash
# ✅ Good
feat/BNCP-234-voice-script-panel
fix/BNCP-189-login-redirect-loop
refactor/BNCP-201-signal-state-migration
chore/BNCP-210-upgrade-angular-17
hotfix/BNCP-299-prod-webhook-timeout

# ❌ Bad
new-feature
johns-branch
fix-bug-2
temp
wip
```

**Rules:**
- Always include ticket ID — links branch to issue tracker
- kebab-case only — no spaces, no underscores, no camelCase
- Description: 3–5 words max
- `hotfix/` bypasses normal review process — use only for production emergencies

---

## 5. Git Flow Strategy

### Trunk-based (recommended for small teams ≤5 devs)

```
main ──────────────────────────────────────► (always deployable)
       │         │              │
   feat/A     feat/B         fix/C
   (1-2 days) (1-2 days)    (hours)
```

- Branch from `main`, merge back to `main` via PR
- Feature branches live max 1–2 days — avoid long-lived branches
- Feature flags for incomplete features in `main`
- Deploy from `main` directly

### Git Flow (for larger teams or scheduled releases)

```
main ────────────────────────────────────────► (production)
      │                          ▲
   release/1.2.0              merge
      │           ▲              │
develop ──────────────────────────────────────► (integration)
      │       │         │
   feat/A   feat/B    fix/C
```

| Branch | Purpose | Merge to |
|---|---|---|
| `main` | Production code only | — |
| `develop` | Integration branch | `main` via release |
| `feat/*` | New features | `develop` |
| `release/*` | Release stabilization | `main` + `develop` |
| `hotfix/*` | Production fixes | `main` + `develop` |

**Choose based on:**
- Small team, continuous deployment → Trunk-based
- Large team, scheduled releases → Git Flow

---

## 6. Git Tag & Release Management

### Semantic Versioning

```
MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH

MAJOR — breaking change (feat! or BREAKING CHANGE footer)
MINOR — new feature     (feat)
PATCH — bug fix         (fix, perf)
```

```bash
# Pre-release
1.0.0-alpha.1
1.0.0-beta.2
1.0.0-rc.1

# Production
1.0.0
1.2.3
```

### Tagging a Release

```bash
# 1. Ensure you're on main and up to date
git checkout <main_branch from .context/git-workflow.md> && git pull

# 2. Create annotated tag (not lightweight)
git tag -a v1.2.0 -m "Release v1.2.0

- feat(channel): add WhatsApp Business integration
- fix(auth): refresh token not cleared on logout
- perf(api): optimize bot list query"

# 3. Push tag
git push origin v1.2.0

# 4. Push all tags at once (if needed)
git push origin --tags
```

**Annotated vs Lightweight tags:**
- `git tag -a` → annotated — has message, tagger, date → **always use this**
- `git tag` → lightweight — just a pointer → avoid for releases

### Tag Naming Rules

```bash
# ✅ Good
v1.0.0
v1.2.3
v2.0.0-beta.1
v2.0.0-rc.1

# ❌ Bad
1.0.0          # missing v prefix
v1.0           # missing patch
release-1.0.0  # wrong format
latest         # not a version
```

### Hotfix Release

```bash
# Branch from the tag that needs fixing
git checkout v1.2.0
git checkout -b hotfix/BNCP-299-webhook-timeout

# Fix, commit, then tag
git tag -a v1.2.1 -m "Hotfix v1.2.1 — webhook timeout on large payloads"
git push origin v1.2.1
```

---

## 7. PR Description Template

```markdown
## Summary
<!-- What does this PR do? 2–3 sentences max. -->

## Changes
<!-- Bullet list of notable changes -->
-
-

## Type of Change
- [ ] feat — new feature
- [ ] fix — bug fix
- [ ] refactor — no behavior change
- [ ] chore / docs / ci

## Testing
- [ ] Unit tests added / updated
- [ ] Tested manually on local
- [ ] Tested on staging

## Screenshots (if UI change)
<!-- Before / After -->

## Related Issues
Closes #
```

---

## 8. Changelog Generation

### Automated (release-please)

```bash
npm install --save-dev release-please

# release-please uses two config files at the repo root:
# release-please-config.json — defines packages and release type
# .release-please-manifest.json — tracks current versions
```

**`release-please-config.json`** — minimal single-package setup:
```json
{
  "$schema": "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/googleapis/release-please/main/schemas/config.json",
  "release-type": "node",
  "packages": {
    ".": {}
  }
}
```

**`.release-please-manifest.json`** — tracks the current version:
```json
{
  ".": "1.2.3"
}
```

**Run locally** (dry-run to preview, then apply):
```bash
# Preview the next release without writing
npx release-please release-pr --repo-url=<owner>/<repo> --dry-run

# Or use the GitHub Action (recommended) — release-please opens a PR automatically
# on every merge to main, bumping the version and updating CHANGELOG.md
```

**What gets included:**

| Commit type | CHANGELOG section | Version bump |
|---|---|---|
| `feat` | Features | Minor |
| `fix` | Bug Fixes | Patch |
| `perf` | Performance | Patch |
| `feat!` / `BREAKING CHANGE` | ⚠ Breaking Changes | Major |
| Others | — (excluded) | — |

### Manual CHANGELOG.md Format

```markdown
# Changelog

## [2.4.0] - 2025-03-18

### ⚠ Breaking Changes
- **api:** remove deprecated v1 endpoints — migrate to /api/v2/*

### Features
- **channel:** add WhatsApp Business integration (#198)
- **bot-builder:** support drag-and-drop node reordering (#201)

### Bug Fixes
- **auth:** refresh token not cleared on logout (#241)
- **flow-editor:** prevent crash on circular nodes (#239)

### Performance
- **api:** reduce bot list query time 60% with index optimization (#237)

---

## [2.3.1] - 2025-03-10
...
```

**Rules:**
- Most recent version at top
- Group by type — Breaking Changes first
- Include PR/issue number on every entry
- Date in ISO format (YYYY-MM-DD)
- Never edit past entries — append only

---

## 9. Quick Reference Card

```
Commit:   <type>(<scope>): <subject>
Branch:   <type>/<ticket-id>-<description>
Tag:      v<MAJOR>.<MINOR>.<PATCH>

Types:    feat | fix | perf | refactor | style | test | docs | chore | ci | revert
Breaking: feat! or BREAKING CHANGE: in footer → major bump

Common scopes (adjust per project):
  auth | api | ui | db | deps | docker | ci

Flow:
  1. Branch from main
  2. Commit with convention
  3. PR → code review
  4. Merge to main
  5. Tag release with annotated tag
  6. Generate / update CHANGELOG.md
```
